tasks.title: Create the audio-to-score orchestrator skill
acceptance_criteria: The skill treats parallel signal extraction as evidence and song form as the first adjudicated musical artifact.
The skill separates preliminary form hypotheses, frozen form, linear source transcription, generated accompaniment, and compressed written form.
The skill defines human approval gates for form, transcription, and accompaniment.
done_definition: SKILL.md is concise, imperative, and routes detailed contracts to references.
agents/openai.yaml matches the final skill description.

tasks.title: Define reusable artifact contracts
acceptance_criteria: Contracts cover source audio, evidence extraction, tempo and meter maps, neutral recurrence groups, form hypotheses, frozen form, melody notes, harmony events, linear score, accompaniment arrangement, written form, approvals, and uncertainty.
Every melody and harmony event can be traced to the linear timeline and an approved form position.
Generated accompaniment remains structurally separate from observed source transcription.
done_definition: Reference files contain concrete JSON examples and invariants without duplicating the core SKILL.md workflow.

tasks.title: Validate final audio-to-score artifacts
acceptance_criteria: Validation rejects missing or unapproved frozen form before notation-ready output.
Validation rejects invalid measure ordering, meter lengths, melody timing, form mappings, generated/source layer mixing, and malformed approval state.
A valid fixture and representative invalid fixtures exercise the validation behavior.
done_definition: The validator runs deterministically with the standard library.

epics.title: Reposition chord recognition as a form-aware module

tasks.title: Update the audio chord recognition skill
acceptance_criteria: The skill no longer claims ownership of the complete audio-to-score workflow.
When invoked inside audio-to-score, chord decisions require approved form coordinates and preserve recurrence-group consensus.
Standalone chord-chart use remains supported with explicit estimated-output language.
done_definition: The skill and its role contracts reference the repository-owned form contract.
Its validator remains compatible with standalone chord analysis.

epics.title: Make repository skills the source of truth

tasks.title: Add explicit skill drift-check and install commands
acceptance_criteria: The command requires a mode and an explicit skill name.
Check mode performs no writes and returns a failing exit status on drift.
Install mode stages and atomically replaces only the named installed skill.
The installed copy records a content hash and repository source path without affecting content comparison.
Traversal, missing source, and non-skill targets are rejected.
done_definition: Temporary-directory tests verify install, clean check, drift detection, and target validation.
Package scripts expose the commands without hiding the required skill name.

tasks.title: Synchronize the completed skills into the installed Codex directory
acceptance_criteria: The repository copies validate before installation.
audio-to-score and audio-chord-recognition install successfully from the repository source.
Post-install check reports no drift for both skills.
done_definition: Installed skill receipts match the repository hashes.
No unrelated installed skill is modified.

assumptions: The first practical score target is a melody-and-chord lead sheet with optional generated piano or guitar accompaniment, not note-for-note polyphonic transcription of every source instrument.
Signal extraction tools produce evidence artifacts but do not need to be implemented in this skill-definition task.
The repository skills directory is the canonical source; the installed Codex directory is a generated deployment target.
No rendered product UI changes are part of this task.
risks: Freezing form too early can hide evidence from melody or harmony, so the contract must distinguish preliminary hypotheses from adjudicated form.
A generated accompaniment could be mistaken for source transcription unless provenance is required per score layer.
Replacing an installed skill can lose direct local edits; the sync command must detect drift and make mutation explicit.
The current active Codex process may require a new session to reload newly installed skill metadata.
